Title: Transgene-Free, Gene-Edited Cavendish Bananas (Musa acuminata, AAA)

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Desaturates phytoene in the carotenoid biosynthesis pathway to produce downstream colored carotenoid intermediates.
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: banana tissues; study assessed embryogenic calli and regenerated plantlets
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: photosynthetic pigment production and plastid/homeostasis functions
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Disruptive tri-allelic knockout caused albino/photobleached plants, indicating loss of carotenoid biosynthesis needed for pigmentation.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. CRISPR/Cas9 transient editing of banana pds generated mono-, bi-, and tri-allelic edits. 2. Albino phenotype was associated with disruptive pds edits, and whole-genome sequencing confirmed tri-allelic pds knockouts in selected lines. 3. Most T-DNA-free pds lines were unedited, but 21.9% of all regenerated lines were T-DNA-free tri-allelic knockouts.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Transient CRISPR/Cas9 knockout; phenotype scoring for albinism; targeted PCR and Sanger sequencing; whole-genome sequencing; T-DNA-specific PCR
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Agrobacterium-mediated transient editing; 48 h kanamycin positive selection; 5-fluorocytosine negative selection during regeneration
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: CaMV 35S constitutive; maize Ubi1 constitutive; rice U3 promoter for gRNA expression
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Complete knockout caused albino plants and loss of normal growth/greenness; one edited line contained vector-derived insertion and was not transgene-free.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful as a visual marker gene to optimize transgene-free editing workflows, but not suitable as a direct nutritional improvement target because knockout causes albino lethality/poor plant quality.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Degree of photobleaching depended on allele dosage: previous and current results support tri-allelic knockouts as white/albino, fewer edited alleles can remain green. One green line (125-4) had tri-allelic edits that likely preserved protein function; a maize polyubiquitin promoter fragment was inserted at the edit site, excluding it from transgene-free status.

Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: lower branch point of carotenoid biosynthesis leading from lycopene to downstream carotenoids
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Product: β-carotene branch carotenoids including beta-carotene and lutein pathway products
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Cyclizes lycopene to form downstream carotenoids such as β-carotene; loss of activity blocks conversion of lycopene into downstream carotenoids.
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: embryogenic calli/embryos and regenerated plant tissues in banana
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: carotenoids support pigmentation and photosystem/plastid homeostasis; lycopene provides pink/red coloration
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Disruptive LCYb editing caused pink calli due to lycopene accumulation; tri-allelic knockout plants became albino with slight pink hue, showing LCYb is essential for downstream carotenoid homeostasis.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Transient CRISPR/Cas9 editing of banana LCYb produced visible pink calli, an early marker of successful disruptive editing due to lycopene accumulation. 2. Mono-, bi-, and tri-allelic LCYb edits were identified, including dropout deletions, indels, and one inversion event. 3. Only tri-allelic LCYb knockouts displayed albino phenotype; 17.6% of regenerated lines were T-DNA-free tri-allelic knockouts.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Transient CRISPR/Cas9 knockout; visual screening of pink calli and albino plants; allele-specific PCR; cloned amplicon Sanger sequencing; whole-genome sequencing; T-DNA-specific PCR
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Agrobacterium-mediated transient editing; 48 h kanamycin positive selection; 5-fluorocytosine negative selection during regeneration
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: CaMV 35S constitutive; maize Ubi1 constitutive; rice U3 promoter for gRNA expression
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Complete knockout caused albinism, indicating severe impairment of photosystem-related metabolism and making full knockout unsuitable for agronomic deployment.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Valuable as an internal visual control for editing platform optimization and as a mechanistic target for altering carotenoid flux, but complete knockout is detrimental; partial or fruit-specific modulation would be needed for nutritional engineering.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: The paper explicitly states LCYb is central at the lower branch point of carotenoid biosynthesis. Down-regulation prevents conversion of lycopene into downstream carotenoids such as α-carotene, β-carotene, and lutein, thereby stimulating lycopene accumulation. Large dropout deletions occurred in 67% of the 12 deeply characterized edited lines.
